{
  "judge_id": "demo-judge",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {

    "R1.7": 2,
    "RX.1": 3,

  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The text mentions one prior work (Sheridan et al., 2013) and contrasts RigorDTI with existing benchmarks like DAVIS and KIBA.",
    "R1.7": "While the text cites Sheridan (2013), it doesn't engage with other potential relevant citations in detail.",
    "RX.1": "The text does not explicitly state uncertainty for any claims, but it does highlight that performance varies significantly under different splitting strategies.",
    "RX.3": "The text clearly focuses on the agreed-upon active set of drug-target interaction (DTI) methods and datasets.",
    "RX.4": "No harmful content or regressions are mentioned in the text.",
    "RX.5": "The text mentions retrained models from released code but doesn't provide specific traceability for all changes."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "R1.7": 0.6,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.5
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The text mentions one prior work (Sheridan et al., 2013) and contrasts RigorDTI with existing benchmarks like DAVIS and KIBA.
rationale.R1.7: While the text cites Sheridan (2013), it doesn't engage with other potential relevant citations in detail.
rationale.RX.1: The text does not explicitly state uncertainty for any claims, but it does highlight that performance varies significantly under different splitting strategies.
rationale.RX.3: The text clearly focuses on the agreed-upon active set of drug-target interaction (DTI) methods and datasets.
rationale.RX.4: No harmful content or regressions are mentioned in the text.
rationale.RX.5: The text mentions retrained models from released code but doesn't provide specific traceability for all changes.
